ORDER ON DEFENDANT WORLD CAMERA, INC'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, TO ARSEL ZAPET ALVARADO, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT WORLD CAMERA, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, TO ARSEL ZAPET ALVARADO, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART, AND IN DENIED IN PART. PURSUANT TO THE AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES, PLAINTIFF SHALL PRODUCE PHONE RECORDS IN A FORM THAT REDACTS PERSONAL INFORMATION BUT PERMITS DEFENDANT TO SEE THE DATES AND DURATIONS OF EACH COMMUNICATION. FOR REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION (RFP) NOS. 20-21 AND 23, PLAINTIFF SHALL PRODUCE ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS IN HER POSSESSION, CUSTODY OR CONTROL RESPONSIVE TO THESE REQUESTS OR STATE SHE HAS NOTHING TO PRODUCE. FOR RFP NO. 27, PLAINTIFF SHALL PRODUCE DOCUMENTS PURSUANT TO HER STIPULATION. ALL OTHER RFPS ARE DENIED. MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. WITHIN 10 DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THE ORDER, PLAINTIFF SHALL SERVE FURTHER VERIFIED RESPONSES AND PRODUCE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT WORLD CAMERA, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO FURTHER RESPOND AND PRODUCE ELECTRONICALLY STORED INFORMATION AND TO IMPOSE MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED, WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE REQUESTED RELIEF IS PREMATURE. PURSUANT TO THE AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES, PLAINTIFF IS PRODUCING HER REDACTED PHONE RECORDS IN RESPONSE TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. THESE RECORDS MAY PROVIDE A BASIS FOR A MOTION DIRECTED TO THE ESI ON THE PHONE ITSELF, OR THEY MAY CUT AGAINST PUTTING THE PARTIES TO THAT EXPENSE AND INCONVENIENCE. AFTER REVIEWING THE PHONE RECORDS, DEFENDANT MAY FILE A MOTION TO PRODUCE ESI IF WARRANTED. DEFENDANT IS ADVISED THAT COST-SHARING FOR THE PRODUCTION OF ESI MAY BE APPROPRIATE SINCE IT WILL REQUIRE TECHNICAL EXPERTISE TO EXTRACT THE ESI AND SEGREGATE RELEVANT MATERIAL FROM IRRELEVANT MATERIAL. MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT WORLD CAMERA, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET NO. ONE, TO ARSEL ZAPET ALVARADO, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 3, 4, 13, 14, 17, 23, 24, 28, 29, 30, 31, 33, 34 - 41, 43, 45, 48, 51 AND 52. DENIED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 5 - 8 AND 10. FOR SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 5 - 8 AND 10, DEFENDANT SEEKS IRRELEVANT INFORMATION DIRECTED TO IMMIGRATION STATUS OR EMPLOYMENT HISTORY. FOR SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 18 AND 19, PLAINTIFF HAS AGREED TO SERVE FURTHER RESPONSES PERTAINING TO THE FOUNDATIONAL INFORMATION RELATING TO PLAINTIFF'S CELL PHONE. FOR SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 4, 13, 23, 28, 31, 34, 36, 38, 40, 43, 45, 48 AND 51, PLAINTIFF SHALL PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES PERTAINING TO THE IDENTIFICATION OF WITNESSES. FOR SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 3, 14, 17, 24, 29, 30, 33, 35, 37, 39, 41, 46, 49 AND 52, PLAINTIFF SHALL PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES BY IDENTIFYING ANY ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS SHE HAS NOT IDENTIFIED ALREADY, AND TO STATE IF SHE CANNOT RESPOND FURTHER. WITHIN 10 DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER, PLAINTIFF SHALL SERVE FURTHER VERIFIED RESPONSES. MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set No. One, To Arsel Zapet Alvarado, And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ALEKSANDR A. VOLKOV, TEL. NO. 987-7000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WORLD CAMERA, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set No. One, To Arsel Zapet Alvarado, And Request For Sanctions is Granted as to Special Interrogatories nos. 3, 4, 13, 14, 17, 23, 24, 28, 29, 30, 31, 33, 34 - 41, 43, 45, 48, 51 and 52. Denied as to Special Interrogatories nos. 5 - 8 and 10. For Special Interrogatories nos. 5 - 8 and 10, Defendant seeks irrelevant information directed to immigration status or employment history. For Special Interrogatories nos. 18 and 19, Plaintiff has agreed to serve further responses pertaining to the foundational information relating to Plaintiff's cell phone. For Special Interrogatories nos. 4, 13, 23, 28, 31, 34, 36, 38, 40, 43, 45, 48 and 51, Plaintiff shall provide further responses pertaining to the identification of witnesses. For Special Interrogatories nos. 3, 14, 17, 24, 29, 30, 33, 35, 37, 39, 41, 46, 49 and 52, Plaintiff shall provide further responses by identifying any additional documents she has not identified already, and to state if she cannot respond further. Within 10 days of service of notice of entry of order, Plaintiff shall serve further verified responses. Monetary sanctions are denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/EHG

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Pltff'S Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, To Arsel Zapet Alvarado, And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ALEKSANDR A. VOLKOV, TEL. NO. 987-7000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WORLD CAMERA, INC.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, To Arsel Zapet Alvarado, And Request For Sanctions IS Granted in part, and in denied in part. Pursuant to the agreement of the parties, Plaintiff shall produce phone records in a form that redacts personal information but permits Defendant to see the dates and durations of each communication. For Requests for Production (RFP) nos. 20-21 and 23, Plaintiff shall produce any responsive documents in her possession, custody or control responsive to these requests or state she has nothing to produce. For RFP no. 27, Plaintiff shall produce documents pursuant to her stipulation. All other RFPs are denied. Monetary sanctions are denied. Within 10 days of service of notice of entry of the order, Plaintiff shall serve further verified responses and produce responsive documents. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/EHG

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Pltff To Further Respond And Produce Electronically Stored Information And To Impose Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-04-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ALEKSANDR A. VOLKOV, TEL. NO. 987-7000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WORLD CAMERA, INC.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Further Respond And Produce Electronically Stored Information And To Impose Monetary Sanctions is Denied, without prejudice. The requested relief is premature. Pursuant to the agreement of the parties, Plaintiff is producing her redacted phone records in response to Defendant's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Requests for Production of Documents. These records may provide a basis for a motion directed to the ESI on the phone itself, or they may cut against putting the parties to that expense and inconvenience. After reviewing the phone records, Defendant may file a motion to produce ESI if warranted. Defendant is advised that cost-sharing for the production of ESI may be appropriate since it will require technical expertise to extract the ESI and segregate relevant material from irrelevant material. Monetary sanctions are denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/EHG
